metadata.role: Data Acquisition Engineer (ETL Extract & Transform specialist)
metadata.location_scope: Americas only (South America, Brazil, US, Canada)
metadata.budget_constraint: startup rates (quote at or below their max posted hourly rate)

client_core_need.summary: Reliable data pipeline: extract auction listings from defined auction-house websites, normalize into consistent structure, keep current. Long-term ongoing project.
client_core_need.specialist_positioning: Data acquisition SPECIALIST (Extract + Transform), NOT full-stack generalist. Depth in scraping, not breadth. Must emphasize scraping expertise, not full-stack.
client_core_need.focus_area: Extract and Transform stages of ETL. Getting structured data reliably out of websites not built to be scraped, keeping it current.

core_responsibilities.catalog_discovery: Detect when new auctions are posted and capture well before sale date. Reliable periodic re-checks are priority, NOT high-frequency real-time polling. Per-lot churn is minor.
core_responsibilities.data_extraction: Extract lot data: lot number, title, description, estimates, images, sale dates, bidding deadlines from auction-house websites with different structures.
core_responsibilities.tool_selection: Use appropriate tools per site: Playwright/Selenium for JS-heavy/dynamic pages, lighter approaches (requests/httpx + BeautifulSoup/lxml/parsel) for static pages. Show judgment, not married to specific library.
core_responsibilities.output: Clean, well-structured JSON with consistent fields across all sources.
core_responsibilities.transformation: Normalization logic that converts inconsistent catalog text into standard fields.
core_responsibilities.operations: Handle pagination, responsible rate-limiting, retries, error logging, per-scraper documentation.
core_responsibilities.respectful_scraping_mandatory: Public pages ONLY. No logging in, no credentials, no circumvention of access controls. Built-in rate-limiting + polite request pacing mandatory. If site has strong anti-scraping protection, flag for API arrangement—DO NOT defeat protection.

required_skills.web_scraping_proven: Demonstrated, real-world scraping of dynamic/JS-heavy sites. Must show examples.
required_skills.toolkit_judgment: Solid command of scraping tools + good judgment about which fits which site. Candidates use: browser-automation (Playwright/Selenium/Puppeteer), scraping frameworks (Scrapy), HTTP fetchers (requests/httpx), parsers (BeautifulSoup/lxml/parsel). Show reasoning.
required_skills.communication: Clear, fluent WRITTEN AND SPOKEN English essential. Work over Slack + video calls. Precise, direct, real-time communication.

nice_to_have.aws: S3, Lambda, basic scheduling (not required, they can handle infra)
nice_to_have.scheduled_jobs: Experience turning scraper into scheduled, repeatable job
nice_to_have.backend_logic: Data matching, notifications (for possible future work, not this posting)

screening_and_application.proposal_must_include: Link to existing scraper code (GitHub/portfolio preferred). If moving forward, there is a small PAID bounded test task: scrape one specific auction page, return clean JSON + approach note.
screening_and_application.interview_format: Video with camera ON. Discuss scraping experience live in English. Talk through past projects in real time. Camera must be on (they do not like camera off; screens out machine translation).

three_required_questions.q1: Describe one dynamic/JS-heavy site you have scraped and how you handled it.
three_required_questions.q2: What is your preferred tech stack for a multi-site scraping pipeline, and why? Show your reasoning, not buzzwords.
three_required_questions.q3: How do you rate-limit and avoid getting blocked?

hard_trip_wire_rule.rule: Proposal MUST start with the word BROCCOLI (no quotes, no asterisks). This is a screening filter. If the cover letter does not begin with BROCCOLI, proposal is filtered out.

michael_positioning.angle_1: Owns the Python backend at U.S. Bank (production ownership = credibility for reliability and maintainability in a scraper pipeline)
michael_positioning.angle_2: Has used Python, Puppeteer, and Playwright to scrape websites before (direct match to their required tools)
michael_positioning.framing: Lead with backend ownership and scraping expertise. Frame the React console as a demo vehicle to show the scraper in action, not the product. The scraper engine is the point.

demo_spec.type: Auction Scraper Console (live multi-site scraper with real accumulation)
key_behaviors: ~3 auction sources, each with different structure, each normalized into one consistent JSON schema
Start Scraper button kicks off the run; results accumulate live in UI as each lot/source processes
Progress indicators, per-source status, counts as scraping happens
Raw/normalized JSON shown in frontend (JSON viewer/inspector alongside rendered cards)
Scraped item images displayed when available
Respectful-scraping story demonstrated in-product: rate-limit pacing, retries, per-source logs, hardened-site→flag-for-API state

design_direction.typography: Serif display (Cormorant/Playfair) + clean sans body (Inter/Source Sans)
design_direction.color_palette: Restrained ivory/charcoal with single refined accent (deep oxblood, gilt/brass, or ink-navy)
design_direction.mood: Premium auction catalog crossed with clean engineering dashboard. Generous whitespace, catalog-card grid with lot numbers.
design_direction.usage: One-pager + deck reuse this language. Bespoke to this client's industry, not portfolio site's.

explicit_hard_constraints: Americas only (South America, Brazil, US, Canada). Reject non-Americas applicants.
Startup budget: quote at or under their max posted hourly rate.
Video interviews camera-on. No camera off (screens out machine translation, ensures fluent English).
No em/en dashes in proposal, cover letter, or deck (hard writing rule).
Respectful scraping ONLY: public pages, no login/credentials/anti-bot circumvention.
If a site is hardened, flag for API arrangement. Do NOT defeat protection.
BROCCOLI first word in cover letter (trip-wire filter).
Must link existing scraper code (GitHub/portfolio) in proposal.

proposal_must: Start with BROCCOLI (first word, no quotes/asterisks)
Name + link the live demo prominently
Emphasize Michael's U.S. Bank Python backend ownership + Python/Puppeteer/Playwright scraping history
Address respectful scraping and rate-limiting explicitly
Position Michael as scraping specialist (not full-stack generalist)
Use bespoke auction-house design (not portfolio's look)
demo_must: Load with no console errors on first paint
Show real scraped JSON + real images
Demonstrate live accumulation of results as scraper runs
Show respectful scraping behavior (rate-limit logs, retries, hardened-site flag state)
Normalize data across 3+ sources into single JSON schema
Use auction-house design aesthetic, not portfolio's
